name: Eilenberg–Ganea Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: The category of simply-connected spaces with finite type — the Eilenberg–Ganea theorem itself as the hermetic seal that closes the relationship between homotopy and homology.

VINIT.justification: Before any structure is imposed, X is void — no dimension, no cohomology, no homotopy groups assigned.

TANCH.justification: The terminal anchor: the complete boundary condition that contains and validates the entire system. It states: if X is simply-connected and cat(X) ≤ n, then X has the homotopy type of an (n+1)-dimensional CW complex.

AFWD.justification: Forward morphism: the directed transformation from a space to its CW model, preserving homotopy type.

AREV.justification: Reverse morphism: the contravariant flow from cohomological dimension constraints back to homotopy-theoretic properties; unwinding the algebraic structure to recover geometric meaning.

CLINK.justification: Sequential chaining: X ≃ Y ≃ Z, where ≃ denotes homotopy equivalence. The composition of two homotopy equivalences is again a homotopy equivalence.

IMSCRIB.justification: Self-reference: the space recognizes itself; the identity homotopy equivalence is the fixed point of the entire construction.

FSPLIT.justification: Co-multiplication: the space X branches into two distinct paths: (1) the categorical dimension cat(X) ≤ n (affirmative), and (2) the existence of an (n+1)-dimensional CW model (affirmative). These are the two arms of the theorem's conclusion.

FFUSE.justification: Multiplication: the two branches (categorical bound and CW dimension) reconstitute the original space X exactly via homotopy equivalence. FFUSE(FSPLIT(X)) = X holds: the CW model is homotopy-equivalent to X, recovering it perfectly.

EVALT.justification: Affirmative evaluation: the Lusternik–Schnirelmann category of X is bounded above by n, confirming the hypothesis of the theorem.

EVALF.justification: Negative evaluation: if the categorical dimension exceeds n, the theorem's conclusion does not apply; the space cannot be modeled by an (n+1)-dimensional CW complex.

ENGAGR.chosen_element: homotopy type and cohomological dimension held simultaneously without resolution
ENGAGR.justification: Paradice: the theorem itself is a paradice — it holds both the homotopy-theoretic property (cat(X) ≤ n) and the homological property (existence of (n+1)-CW model) in simultaneous, unresolved duality. Neither reduces to the other; both are true at once.

IFIX.justification: ROM fixation: once a CW model is constructed for X, it is append-only and irreversible. The cellular decomposition is a permanent, immutable record of the homotopy type.

phase_2.split_input: simply-connected space X with cat(X) ≤ n
split_outputs: T-arm: categorical dimension bound cat(X) ≤ n (EVALT)
F-arm: absence of lower-dimensional CW model (EVALF / AREV)
phase_2.fuse_element: homotopy type reconstruction via CW model
phase_2.fuse_result: X ≃ CW complex of dimension ≤ n+1 (homotopy equivalent to original X)

phase_3.void_description: 00 — X is unspecified; no homotopy type, no categorical dimension, no CW structure assigned.
phase_3.true_description: 01 — X is simply-connected, cat(X) ≤ n, and admits an (n+1)-dimensional CW model homotopy-equivalent to X. The theorem's conclusion holds.
phase_3.false_description: 10 — X fails the hypothesis (either not simply-connected, or cat(X) > n); the theorem does not apply.
phase_3.both_description: 11 — Paradice: the homotopy type and cohomological dimension are held simultaneously in unresolved duality. The theorem itself is this paradice: it asserts that two seemingly different properties (categorical and homological) are equivalent without reducing one to the other.

steps.domain_action: unspecified space X — void state, no structure
Eilenberg–Ganea theorem boundary — the complete statement that closes the system
homotopy equivalence X → CW model — forward morphism toward the cellular structure
cohomological descent — reverse flow from algebraic constraints back to geometric meaning
composition of homotopy equivalences — chaining X ≃ Y ≃ Z
identity map id_X — self-recognition of the space
Lusternik–Schnirelmann category splitting — branches into (cat(X) ≤ n) and (CW dimension ≤ n+1)
cat(X) ≤ n is satisfied — affirmative branch, hypothesis confirmed
forward morphism along T-arm — construction of CW model respecting categorical bound
homotopy type reconstruction — T-arm and F-arm reconstitute X exactly
cat(X) > n fails — negative branch, theorem does not apply
reverse morphism along F-arm — descent from failed hypothesis
homotopy type and cohomological dimension held simultaneously — paradice of the theorem
CW complex structure fixed permanently — append-only cellular decomposition recorded
composition of all equivalences — the full chain of homotopy equivalences
identity map id_X again — self-reference loop closes
theorem boundary affirmed — the system is sealed

phase_5.compiler_frontend: Algebraic topology: translates homotopy-theoretic intentions (cat(X) ≤ n) into cellular constructions (CW complex of dimension n+1).
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Homotopy equivalence ≃ — the communication protocol between homotopy groups and homology groups; they speak the same language via the CW model.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: The CW complex structure — cells, attaching maps, and the cellular chain complex form the persistent state store.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: Inductive cellular construction — cells are attached dimension by dimension, ordered by skeletal level.
phase_5.alfs_store: The Eilenberg–Ganea theorem itself — the canonical reference that bootstraps all homotopy-to-homology translations.

phase_6.cycle_cost: Algebraic computation: each homotopy group calculation and each cellular attachment consumes algebraic operations (homology computation, chain complex reduction).
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: Simply-connected space X with unknown CW structure; homotopy groups and homology groups are separate, unrelated.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: X is now modeled by an explicit (n+1)-dimensional CW complex; homotopy and homology are unified via cellular homology.
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The cycle is entropy-conservative: no information is lost. The homotopy type is preserved exactly (X ≃ CW model), and the categorical dimension bound is maintained. The theorem is a perfect reversible transformation: homotopy ↔ homology via CW structure.

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → TANCH → AFWD → AREV → CLINK → IMSCRIB → FSPLIT → EVALT → AFWD → FFUSE → EVALF → AREV → ENGAGR → IFIX → CLINK → IMSCRIB → TANCH
-- Class: Eilenberg–Ganea Theorem
-- Fingerprint: sig=(11,2,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=17
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(6, 9)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑡  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑼 → 𐑾  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [2] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑡 → 𐑗  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [3] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑾 → 𐑱  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [4] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑗 → 𐑠  | composition — regime coherence
--   [5] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑱 → 𐑚  | identity — self-imscription
--   [6] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [7] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [8] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑙  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [9] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑖  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [10] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑙 → 𐑗  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [11] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑖 → 𐑳  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [12] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑗 → 𐑭  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [13] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑳 → 𐑱  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [14] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑭 → 𐑠  | composition — regime coherence
--   [15] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑱 → 𐑡  | identity — self-imscription
--   [16] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑠 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def eilenberg_ganea_theorem_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑡)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (unspecified space X — void state, no structure)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [1] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Eilenberg–Ganea theorem boundary — the complete statement that closes the system)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑡 𐑗)  -- [2] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (homotopy equivalence X → CW model — forward morphism toward the cellular stru...)
  (.arrow 𐑗 𐑾 𐑱)  -- [3] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (cohomological descent — reverse flow from algebraic constraints back to geome...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑗 𐑠)  -- [4] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (composition of homotopy equivalences — chaining X ≃ Y ≃ Z)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑱 𐑚)  -- [5] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (identity map id_X — self-recognition of the space)
  -- FSPLIT [6] (gran := 𐑚) (Lusternik–Schnirelmann category splitting — branches into (cat(X) ≤ n) and (CW dimension ≤ n+1)) / FFUSE [9] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (cat(X) ≤ n is satisfied — affirmative branch, hypothesis confirmed)
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [8] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (forward morphism along T-arm — construction of CW model respecting categorica...)
      -- F-branch (0 nodes)
      (.refl 𐑙))  -- F-branch: empty arc (direct to FFUSE.F)
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [9]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑖)  -- [9] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (homotopy type reconstruction — T-arm and F-arm reconstitute X exactly)
  (.arrow 𐑖 𐑙 𐑗)  -- [10] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (cat(X) > n fails — negative branch, theorem does not apply)
  (.arrow 𐑗 𐑖 𐑳)  -- [11] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (reverse morphism along F-arm — descent from failed hypothesis)
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑗 𐑭)  -- [12] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (homotopy type and cohomological dimension held simultaneously — paradice of t...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑳 𐑱)  -- [13] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (CW complex structure fixed permanently — append-only cellular decomposition r...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑭 𐑠)  -- [14] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (composition of all equivalences — the full chain of homotopy equivalences)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑱 𐑡)  -- [15] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (identity map id_X again — self-reference loop closes)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑠 𐑼)  -- [16] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (theorem boundary affirmed — the system is sealed)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def eilenberg_ganea_theorem_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (eilenberg_ganea_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def eilenberg_ganea_theorem_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (eilenberg_ganea_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem eilenberg_ganea_theorem_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
